beginnerGuide.title: CookieRun: OvenSmash Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.intro: CookieRun: OvenSmash is a fast real-time arena game where short matches are decided by movement, timing, positioning, and objective control. The easiest way to improve early is to understand what each mode asks you to do, pick one Cookie to learn properly, and treat ultimates and spell cards as planned tools instead of panic buttons.

steps.title: Understand the CookieRun: OvenSmash match loop first
steps.description: OvenSmash is built around short, tactical arena battles. You control one Cookie at a time, and good movement, ability timing, and teamwork matter more than long grinding sessions.
highlights: Real-time multiplayer arena combat
Short and dynamic matches
Unique Cookie skills and ultimates
Spell cards that can swing fights
Objective-based modes instead of pure kill chasing

steps.title: Play the objective, not just the fight
steps.description: Different modes reward different kinds of pressure. Early losses often happen because new players chase eliminations and ignore the actual win condition.
highlights: Escort the Turtle to the enemy castle or destroy the castle
Capture the center stage to score over time
Collect Jellies and deposit them in the enemy goal
Reach the target elimination count before the opposing team
Be the last player standing in the free-for-all mode
Collect gnomes, build structures, and score points

steps.title: Pick one main Cookie before branching out
steps.description: A strong early habit is to stay on one Cookie long enough to learn attack range, cooldown rhythm, and ultimate timing. Keep one backup pick, but do not swap constantly.
highlights: Learn one main first
Keep a secondary pick for bad matchups or team needs
Do not switch Cookies every few matches
Mechanical comfort is worth more than shallow roster coverage

steps.title: Know your team role in CookieRun: OvenSmash 3v3
steps.description: Beginners perform better when they understand how their Cookie fits into the team. Balanced lineups are usually more consistent than all-damage teams.
highlights: Tanks and frontliners create space and absorb pressure
Damage dealers finish fights and punish bad positioning
Supports keep teammates alive or increase team pressure
Stay near teammates in 3v3 instead of forcing solo fights

steps.title: Use spell cards and ultimates at the right moment
steps.description: Many matches turn on one well-timed shield, heal, freeze, or burst ultimate. Strong timing beats random button use.
highlights: Save big tools for team fights or objective pushes
Use shields before impact, not after you are already collapsing
Use healing when it changes a fight, not after the fight is lost
Cast ultimates when enemies group up or commit to an objective

steps.title: Build progress through consistency
steps.description: Progress comes from steady play and smarter resource use, not from trying to unlock everything immediately. Daily missions and mode variety help you grow faster.
highlights: Complete daily missions
Play different modes to learn map flow
Gradually unlock stronger Cookies and spell cards
Avoid common mistakes: over-aggression, ignoring objectives, wasting ultimates, and switching mains too often

quickTips: Movement and positioning decide more fights than raw damage stats.
Short matches mean every decision counts from the opening seconds.
Spell cards are planned tools, not panic buttons.
Consistent play on one Cookie builds skill faster than roster-hopping.

codes.title: CookieRun: OvenSmash Codes
codes.intro: Coupon codes are one of the fastest early sources of free Crystals in CookieRun: OvenSmash. The official redemption flow uses the DevPlay coupon page, and the reward arrives through your in-game mailbox after you claim the code and restart the game.

expiredCodes.title: Expired CookieRun: OvenSmash Codes
expiredCodes.emptyState: No expired CookieRun: OvenSmash codes are listed on the current March 2026 trackers.

redeemSteps.title: How to Redeem CookieRun: OvenSmash Codes

entries.instruction: Launch CookieRun: OvenSmash and complete the tutorial.
Open the menu, go to Settings, then open the Account tab.
Check the DevPlay account information used for the game.
Open the official coupon page at coupon.devplay.com.
Enter your DevPlay Account and the coupon code.
Press Claim Rewards, then restart the game and collect the reward from your mailbox.

entries.purpose: The account tied to your game login
The redeem code you want to claim

tierList.title: CookieRun: OvenSmash Tier List
tierList.intro: Tier lists matter more in CookieRun: OvenSmash than in a slow PvE game because your first main shapes your early win rate, resource use, and comfort with the mode rotation. A good first pick should match your preferred role, help in multiple game types, and be worth early upgrade investment.

tiers.summary: Top launch-meta picks with strong all-around value, role impact, or easy conversion into wins.
featuredNotes: Dark Choco Cookie stands out because he can tank, deal damage, and sustain well.
Red Velvet Cookie is valued for displacement and fight control.
Cherry Cookie is a free starter who still performs at a top-tier level.

tiers.summary: Strong picks that reward better positioning, cleaner execution, or a more stable frontline around them.
featuredNotes: Ice Pop Cookie brings excellent crowd control but wants better team spacing.
Blueberry Pie Cookie is a mage who can play aggressively without feeling paper-thin.
Chili Pepper Cookie can clean up fights well if your entry timing is good.

tiers.summary: Playable options with narrower value, simpler kits, or more matchup-dependent performance.
featuredNotes: GingerBrave is straightforward and flexible, but not as impactful as the higher tiers.
Fruit Punch Cookie works, but stronger frontline options are available.
Tiger Lily Cookie needs more setup and feels better later into her progression.

bestBuilds.title: CookieRun: OvenSmash Best Builds
bestBuilds.intro: In CookieRun: OvenSmash, a build is not just about raw damage. Your special variant, ultimate version, and Power Biscuit choices all shape how your Cookie enters fights, exits fights, and supports the team objective. The strongest early results usually come from pairing one stable main with disciplined resource spending.

playstyle: Poke with your basic attack before committing.
Dash in, use your ultimate, then dash back out.
Prioritize backline targets and low-HP enemies.
Pair well with a distracting frontliner such as Dark Choco Cookie.
Go in with teammates, apply crowd control, and disengage before enemies unfreeze.
Do not frontline alone like a tank or bruiser.
Play beside a sturdier teammate in team fights.
For a more support-heavy style, Freezing Bat Flip is a strong alternative ultimate.

coreRules: Invest in one main first because Coins are needed in large amounts.
Farm Crystals through quests, events, and redeem codes.
Farm Coins through Cookie Mastery, events, and reward boxes.

playstyle: Use one stable main to learn ranges, cooldowns, and objective timing before spreading upgrades.
Build depth on a proven main first, then add a backup pick.

cookiesList.title: CookieRun: OvenSmash Cookies List
cookiesList.subtitle: Browse the current playable roster by rarity, combat role, and practical battlefield job. This module is built for quick lookup after pulls, unlocks, or ranked losses.
cookiesList.intro: CookieRun: OvenSmash mixes Tanks, Bruisers, Controllers, Supports, Marksmen, Mages, Assassins, and Bombers across a launch roster of familiar and new Cookies. The fastest way to use this list is to match a Cookie's role with the job your team is missing: engage, sustained damage, control, or healing.

cookies.name: Madeleine Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Lead teamfights, start objective contests, and protect ranged carries

cookies.name: Red Velvet Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Create picks, drag enemies out of position, and punish clustered teams

cookies.name: String Cheese Cookie
cookies.signature: Safe ranged poke with turret support
cookies.bestUse: Hold backline angles, pressure lanes, and chip enemies before commits

cookies.name: Ice Pop Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Lock down targets for follow-up damage and stall tight choke fights

cookies.name: Jerky Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Punish grouped enemies and add delayed area pressure in midrange fights

cookies.name: Bacon Roll Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Dive into skirmishes, chase weakened targets, and brawl around objectives

cookies.name: Fruit Punch Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Frontline for beginner-friendly teams and soak pressure in grouped fights

cookies.name: Melon Soda Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Stabilize teamfights, help carries stay active, and round out balanced comps

cookies.name: Dark Choco Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Trade aggressively, survive extended fights, and pressure frontline mirrors

cookies.name: Strawberry Crepe Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Block space, set up for teammates, and slow enemy entries into objectives

cookies.name: Peach Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Punish isolated backliners and snowball after enemies spend cooldowns

cookies.name: Tiger Lily Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Harass safely, then collapse when a low target is exposed

cookies.name: Blueberry Pie Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Reposition around terrain and threaten midrange burst from unusual lines

cookies.name: Latte Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Control space with magic damage and support slower, structured teamfights

cookies.name: Peppermint Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Keep frontline alive, extend fights, and anchor balanced 3v3 comps

cookies.name: Rye Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Deal steady backline damage and punish enemies who walk predictable routes

cookies.name: Blue Slushy Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Play open maps, fire through lines, and wear teams down before all-ins

cookies.name: Cherry Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Throw pressure over walls, defend objectives, and disrupt enemy pathing

cookies.name: Chili Pepper Cookie
cookies.bestUse: Flank exposed targets, finish low enemies, and punish weak backlines

cookies.name: GingerBrave
cookies.bestUse: Learn engage timing, survive close combat, and fill gaps in casual lineups

gameModes.title: CookieRun: OvenSmash Game Modes Guide
gameModes.subtitle: Learn what each current mode asks you to do and what kind of team habits convert more matches into wins. The goal here is not just knowing the rule, but knowing the win pattern.
gameModes.intro: OvenSmash is not a one-plan game. Some modes reward hard engage and area control, while others reward safe rotations, pick pressure, or independent survival. Treat each queue like a different objective puzzle and your results improve much faster.

modes.name: Castle Crasher
modes.objective: Capture the turtle and escort it toward the enemy sandcastle.
modes.howToWin: Control space around the turtle, clear defenders before overextending, and finish pushes with numbers advantage.
modes.bestFor: Teams with one sturdy frontliner and at least one ranged follow-up damage dealer

modes.name: Drop the Beat
modes.objective: Hold the center stage to earn points over time until your team reaches the target score.
modes.howToWin: Take first control with engage tools, then cycle in and out of the point instead of stacking in one spot.
modes.bestFor: Comps that can contest mid repeatedly and punish enemy over-commits

modes.name: Battle Royale / Wild Royale
modes.objective: Outlast every other Cookie while collecting power resources and avoiding map hazards.
modes.howToWin: Farm safely early, keep track of shrinking safe space, and only take fights that improve your final-position odds.
modes.bestFor: Self-sufficient duelists and players comfortable rotating alone

modes.name: Gnome Battle
modes.objective: Gather Sugar Gnomes to build your team sculpture before the enemy completes theirs.
modes.howToWin: Escort carriers, steal enemy gnomes when possible, and reset quickly after losing control of the center route.
modes.bestFor: Mobile teams that can rotate fast and defend turn-ins under pressure

modes.name: Jelly Deposit
modes.objective: Collect Jellies across the map and deposit them in the enemy goal to earn points.
modes.howToWin: Open a scoring path first, then move carriers in waves instead of trickling resources one by one.
modes.bestFor: Teams with speed, peel, and strong re-entry after short skirmishes

modes.name: Smash Arena
modes.objective: Defeat enemies until your team reaches the required elimination count first.
modes.howToWin: Take clean 3v2 or 3v1 trades, disengage from bad fights early, and never donate free deaths while regrouping.
modes.bestFor: Direct combat teams with reliable engage, burst, or strong pick tools

currencyGuide.title: CookieRun: OvenSmash Currency Guide
currencyGuide.subtitle: Use this module to understand which currencies matter most, how they enter your account, and what they should be spent on first. Good resource habits speed up both roster growth and match performance.
currencyGuide.intro: The biggest early trap in OvenSmash is spreading resources across too many Cookies too fast. Your best early value usually comes from strengthening a small core roster, using premium resources for real progression, and treating cosmetics and wide roster spending as later goals.

currencies.name: Coins
currencies.howToGet: Matches, tasks, Progress Level rewards, Coin Boxes, Battle Pass, Oven Crown Road, login missions, and events
currencies.bestFirstUse: Level up one or two main Cookies instead of your whole roster
currencies.mainUses: Cookie leveling, upgrades, Power Biscuits, and some Common Soulchips

currencies.name: Crystals
currencies.howToGet: Matches, missions, event tasks, Battle Pass, Progress Level rewards, and shop packs
currencies.bestFirstUse: Target meaningful roster progress such as Epic Soulchips or high-value shop items
currencies.mainUses: Shop purchases, Epic Soulchips, and Universal Soulchips

currencies.name: Soulchips
currencies.howToGet: Match missions, Oven Crown Road, Battle Pass, and shop purchases
currencies.bestFirstUse: Finish unlocks or upgrades for the Cookies you actually queue with
currencies.mainUses: Unlocking new Cookies and powering up owned Cookies

currencies.name: General Summon Tickets
currencies.howToGet: Oven Crown Road rewards, events, and code-style giveaway rewards
currencies.bestFirstUse: Expand your core options before chasing duplicates for niche picks
currencies.mainUses: Summon Shop pulls for Cookies and related rewards

currencies.name: Rainbow Credits
currencies.howToGet: Mostly shop purchases, with smaller amounts available through the Battle Pass
currencies.bestFirstUse: Save for looks you actually plan to use often
currencies.mainUses: Costume Summon Shop for clothing and fashion sets

currencies.name: Oven Crowns
currencies.howToGet: Winning battles across the game's different modes
currencies.bestFirstUse: Push reward milestones consistently instead of stopping after a few wins
currencies.mainUses: Advancing the Oven Crown Road for Soulchip packs, tickets, boxes, and other progression rewards

spendingTips: Invest Coins into one or two main Cookies before spreading across the roster.
Save Crystals for meaningful progression like Epic Soulchips, not impulse shop buys.
Use coupon codes immediately to maximize early Crystal income.
Push Oven Crown Road milestones consistently for the best free-to-play reward flow.
Treat cosmetic spending as a later goal after your core roster is upgraded.

rolesGuide.title: CookieRun: OvenSmash Roles Guide
rolesGuide.subtitle: Understand what each broad team job is supposed to do in a match and which Cookies commonly fill that job. This module is meant to help players pick a role identity before spending upgrades.
rolesGuide.intro: The cleanest way to build better teams in OvenSmash is to think in jobs, not just favorite characters. A strong comp usually needs someone to start fights, someone to finish damage, someone to disrupt or lock targets, and someone to keep the team stable through repeated skirmishes.

roles.name: Frontline
roles.summary: Frontline picks are your Tanks and Bruisers. They take first contact, claim space, and make it safer for the rest of the team to function.
responsibilities: Start fights when your team is in follow-up range
Absorb pressure and block direct access to your backline
Stand on high-value objectives first and force enemies to walk through you
roles.playTip: Do not engage alone just because you can survive for a few seconds. Engage when your damage dealers are ready to cash in.

roles.name: Damage
roles.summary: Damage picks convert openings into kills, objective clears, and score swings. This group includes Marksmen, Mages, Assassins, and Bombers.
responsibilities: Pressure enemies after frontline contact starts
Punish exposed targets and bad positioning quickly
Keep steady output during extended teamfights and objective contests
roles.playTip: Most damage losses come from stepping too far forward too early. Let your frontline or controller create the opening first.

roles.name: Control
roles.summary: Control picks win fights by freezing, pulling, knocking up, slowing, or otherwise breaking enemy timing. They turn even fights into uneven ones.
responsibilities: Interrupt enemy engages or punish stacked movement
Create picks before full teamfights begin
Lock important targets long enough for your team to finish them
roles.playTip: Your best value usually comes from controlling the enemy carry or the diver reaching your backline, not from randomly spending crowd control on tanks.

roles.name: Support
roles.summary: Support picks keep your team stable through healing, buffs, utility, and safer fight resets. They are the glue in longer objective games.
responsibilities: Heal or stabilize allies before and after trades
Boost the team's uptime so carries can keep firing
Help winning fights stay won by preventing a sudden collapse
roles.playTip: Stand close enough to influence the fight, but far enough that the enemy cannot remove your healing or utility first.

ovenCrownRoad.title: CookieRun: OvenSmash Oven Crown Road Guide
ovenCrownRoad.intro: Oven Crown Road is the game's long-term reward track. Winning more matches pushes your total Oven Crowns higher and unlocks soulchips, summon tickets, boxes, and more. The published reward track currently runs up to 56,800 Crowns with rank bands from Bronze to Challenger.

steps.title: Understand what counts toward the Road
steps.description: Oven Crown Road progress comes from the total Oven Crowns you earn by winning battles. The more wins you stack, the faster you unlock Road rewards.
details: Crowns are earned through battle wins.
Progress is based on total Oven Crowns earned.
The reward track is shared across your overall progression rather than tied to one single Cookie.

steps.title: Know the rank ladder before you push
steps.description: The Road is divided into Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond, Master, and Challenger, each with sub-ranks. Early ranks mainly hand out soulchip materials, while later ranks shift into repeatable ticket milestones.
details: Bronze I-V
Silver I-V
Gold I-V
Platinum I-V
Diamond I-V
Master I-V
Challenger I-V

steps.title: Clear the early Bronze and Silver milestones
steps.description: The first part of the Road is packed with soulchip materials that help your roster come online quickly.
details: 0 Crowns: Bronze I — 80 Cherry Cookie's Soulchips
100 Crowns: Bronze II — 160 Common Soulchip Choice Boxes
250 Crowns: Bronze III — 80 Rare Soulchip Choice Packs
500 Crowns: Bronze IV — 40 Epic Soulchip Choice Packs
750 Crowns: Bronze V — 240 Common Soulchip Choice Boxes
1,000 Crowns: Silver I — 120 Rare Soulchip Choice Boxes
1,250 Crowns: Silver II — 60 Epic Soulchip Choice Boxes
1,500 Crowns: Silver III — 320 Common Soulchip Choice Packs
1,750 Crowns: Silver IV — 160 Rare Soulchip Choice Packs
2,000 Crowns: Silver V — 80 Rare Soulchip Choice Packs

steps.title: Use Gold and Platinum to stock summon resources
steps.description: From Gold onward, the Road alternates between Soulchip Remix Tickets and General Summon Tickets, making this the first major pull-resource stretch.
details: 2,300 Crowns: Gold I — 1 Soulchip Remix Ticket
3,000 Crowns: Gold II — 5 General Summon Tickets
4,000 Crowns: Gold III — 1 Soulchip Remix Ticket
5,000 Crowns: Gold IV — 5 General Summon Tickets
6,000 Crowns: Gold V — 1 Soulchip Remix Ticket
7,000 Crowns: Platinum I — 5 General Summon Tickets
8,000 Crowns: Platinum II — 1 Soulchip Remix Ticket
9,000 Crowns: Platinum III — 5 General Summon Tickets
10,000 Crowns: Platinum IV — 1 Soulchip Remix Ticket
11,000 Crowns: Platinum V — 5 General Summon Tickets

steps.title: Plan the long climb through Diamond, Master, and Challenger
steps.description: The published late-game breakdown compresses each band into milestone ranges, and each listed sub-rank milestone in these bands awards a Soulchip Remix Ticket.
details: 12,000 to 24,000 Crowns: Diamond I to Diamond V — 1 Soulchip Remix Ticket at the listed milestones
27,000 to 39,000 Crowns: Master I to Master V — 1 Soulchip Remix Ticket at the listed milestones
42,000 to 54,000 Crowns: Challenger I to Challenger V — 1 Soulchip Remix Ticket at the listed milestones
The current published Road endpoint is 56,800 Oven Crowns.

steps.title: Push smarter, not just longer
steps.description: Win consistently, stick to a small core roster, and avoid tilt. That matters more than constantly swapping characters while you are still learning match flow.
details: Focus on wins, because wins are what move the Road forward.
Master a few strong Cookies first instead of spreading resources too thin.
Build around team synergy so your matches are easier to close out.
Take a break after a losing streak to protect your momentum.

summonGuide.title: CookieRun: OvenSmash Summon Guide
summonGuide.subtitle: OvenSmash spreads roster growth across multiple systems instead of one single gacha. This guide groups the official probability tables into the parts that matter most for account growth.
summonGuide.intro: Your roster power mainly comes from soulchips, coins, and pull tickets. The official probability pages cover Soulchip Remix, General Summon, Costume Draw, Battle Boxes, and Coin Boxes, while the live coupon page ecosystem can also add extra Crystals for more pulls.

summons.name: General Summon
summons.summary: The baseline soulchip pull. Official rates split the result into Common 40%, Rare 40%, and Epic 20%.
details: Common total rate: 40.0000%; possible amounts include 40-59, 60-79, 80-119, 120-159, and 160.
Rare total rate: 40.0000%; possible amounts include 20-29, 30-39, 40-59, 60-79, and 80.
Epic total rate: 20.0000%; possible amounts include 5, 6-8, 9-12, 20, and 40.
Use this when you want broad roster growth across multiple grades instead of a single high-roll target.

summons.name: Soulchip Remix
summons.summary: A ticket-based progression pull that always pays out Common, Rare, Epic soulchips, and Coins together, with five official stages.
details: This is the cleanest system for converting a single ticket into both soulchip progress and coin income.

summons.name: Costume Draw
summons.summary: The cosmetic system uses four official stage buckets, including standard Epic and themed-only Epic outcomes.
details: Stage 1 Common Grade: 49.8421%
Stage 2 Rare Grade: 33.7587%
Stage 3 Epic Grade, including Themed and Standard: 10.9077%
Stage 4 Epic Grade, themed only and including Full Set chance: 5.4916%
At Stages 1-2, Rainbow Mileage may also appear and is classified as Common grade.

summons.name: Battle Boxes
summons.summary: Battle Boxes are another major progression source because they roll both upgrade tiers and tier-specific rewards.
details: Battle Box III starts at Lv3 80.0000% and can reach Lv6 1.5000%.
Battle Box V gives Lv5 80.0000% and Lv6 20.0000%.
Battle Box VI upgrades to Lv6 100.0000%.
Lv1 rewards include Common Random Soulchips x20 at 50.0000%, Rare Random Soulchips x10 at 20.0000%, or Coins x1,000 at 30.0000%.
Lv6 rewards include Epic Random Soulchips x40 at 30.0000%, Coins x10,000 at 30.0000%, Rare Universal Soulchips x40 at 10.0000%, or Epic Universal Soulchips x20 at 30.0000%.

summons.name: Coin Boxes
summons.summary: Coin Boxes are the cleanest pure-currency source in the official probability tables.

summons.name: Extra Pull Resources
summons.summary: Launch-period codes can add extra Crystals that loop back into your broader pull economy.
details: Active code checked on March 29, 2026: WELCOME2OVENSMASH — 1,000 Crystals.
Coupon redemption is handled through the official DevPlay coupon page.
Redeemed rewards are sent to your in-game mailbox.

inviteCodes.title: CookieRun: OvenSmash Invite Codes
inviteCodes.subtitle: This launch event is built around Friendship Points. Your Player ID functions as the invite code, and both milestone rewards and daily quests feed the same point track.
inviteCodes.intro: Invite Codes in CookieRun: OvenSmash are not fixed universal coupons. They are player-specific invite entries tied to the Invite a Friend event, and the fastest way to finish the reward track is to combine successful invites with the daily Friendship Point quests.

items.title: Your invite code
items.reward: Used by another player to enter the Invite a Friend event
notes: The shared invite code is your Player ID.
When another player enters your code, that player receives 200 Friendship Points.

items.title: Successful invite bonus
items.code: One friend enters your Player ID
notes: You receive 500 Friendship Points per successful invite.
This can be done up to 5 times.

items.title: Daily quest route
items.reward: Up to 200 Friendship Points per day
notes: Play 1 time while at a party with a friend: 40 Friendship Points
Share your invitation code 1 time: 40 Friendship Points
Play Standard Mode 3 times: 20 Friendship Points

items.title: Milestone: 200 Friendship Points
notes: This is the first point-track reward.
A single entered invite code is enough to pass this threshold for the invited player.

items.title: Milestone: 600 Friendship Points
notes: This is the second reward breakpoint on the event track.

items.title: Milestone: 1,000 Friendship Points
notes: This is the first cosmetic reward on the Invite a Friend track.

items.title: Milestone: 1,500 Friendship Points
notes: This breakpoint adds direct upgrade value on top of the earlier cosmetic milestone.

items.title: Milestone: 2,500 Friendship Points
notes: This is the top listed event milestone in the published reward breakdown.

friendsParty.title: CookieRun: OvenSmash Friends & Party Guide
friendsParty.subtitle: The friend list and the party lobby are separate UI flows in CookieRun: OvenSmash. Once you know where each menu lives, grouping up is quick and repeatable.
friendsParty.intro: CookieRun: OvenSmash does not put friend adding and party creation in the same place, which is why many first-time players miss one of the two systems. Use the Friends menu for account connections and the Party tab for queueing together with up to three players.

steps.title: Go to the Plaza first
steps.description: The friend flow starts from the Plaza, which is the main interface area. The Friends icon sits next to the chat rather than inside the Party menu.

steps.title: Open the Friends menu
steps.description: Tap the Friends icon beside chat while you are in the Plaza. This is the correct place to manage friends and send requests.

steps.title: Add by nickname or Player ID
steps.description: You can add someone by entering either their nickname or their Player ID in the search bar at the top of the Friends screen.

steps.title: Use the Add Friend tab for recent players
steps.description: If you already played with or against someone, the Add Friend tab can surface those recent players so you do not need to type their information manually.

steps.title: Create a party from the Party tab
steps.description: The Party menu is for grouped queueing, not for adding friends. Press Create Party to open your own lobby.

steps.title: Party size is capped at three players
steps.description: A party can include up to three players. The party leader handles the queue once the group is assembled.

steps.title: Join another group with a Party Code
steps.description: If a friend already made the lobby, press Join and type in their Party Code to enter that party.

steps.title: Copy or share the code from the party screen
steps.description: Each party has a unique code. Once the party is created, you can copy the code from the bottom of the screen and send it to your teammates.

steps.title: Use parties for social event progress too
steps.description: The Invite a Friend event includes a daily quest for playing one time while at a party with a friend, so grouping up also feeds Friendship Point progress.

ratesGuide.title: CookieRun: OvenSmash Rates Guide
ratesGuide.subtitle: Read the official summon, costume draw, and box probabilities in a cleaner format. Compare value before spending Crystals, tickets, or boxes.
ratesGuide.intro: CookieRun: OvenSmash publishes separate probability tables for General Summon, Soulchip Remix, Costume Draw, Battle Boxes, and Coin Boxes. General Summon splits 40% Common, 40% Rare, and 20% Epic by total grade rate, while Costume Draw is weighted toward Common and Rare looks with a smaller Epic slice.

costumes.title: CookieRun: OvenSmash Costumes Guide
costumes.subtitle: See how OvenSmash handles style, themed costume drops, and visual collections. Covers what is cosmetic, how the costume pool is structured, and what players can chase.
costumes.intro: Customization is a core part of OvenSmash's identity. Players can build a Cookie with dozens of looks and items, while the official costume channels feature named seasonal themes such as Summer Time Breeze and Cosmic Odyssey.

items.name: CookieRun: OvenSmash Custom Looks
items.description: The App Store description presents customization as a headline feature, stating that players can create their own Cookie style with dozens of looks and items.
items.howToGet: Use the game's appearance and costume systems to change your Cookie's visual style.
items.playerTakeaway: The broadest style layer in OvenSmash, aimed at self-expression rather than raw combat power.

items.name: CookieRun: OvenSmash Standard Costume Pool
items.description: The official Costume Draw table confirms a mixed pool that includes Common, Rare, and Epic costume results, with Stage 3 listing Epic results that include both themed and standard costumes.
items.playerTakeaway: Most costume results sit in the Common and Rare ranges, while Epic results are a smaller part of the pool.

items.name: CookieRun: OvenSmash Themed Epic Pool
items.description: Stage 4 of Costume Draw is reserved for Epic Grade themed-only results and also includes a full set chance.
items.howToGet: Pull through the top themed Epic stage in Costume Draw.
items.playerTakeaway: The cleanest chase lane for players who want event-style costume sets rather than general cosmetic variety.

items.name: CookieRun: OvenSmash Summer Time Breeze
items.description: A summer costume line built around breezy outfits, stylish shades, and a vacation look for the Cookies.
items.howToGet: Watch the official promotion cycle and matching costume releases.
items.playerTakeaway: Leans into bright seasonal fashion and works well as a showcase set for style-first players.

items.name: CookieRun: OvenSmash Cosmic Odyssey
items.description: A space-themed costume line described with phrases such as space hackers and galactic wanderers.
items.howToGet: Watch the official promotion cycle and matching costume releases.
items.playerTakeaway: Gives OvenSmash a stronger sci-fi urban fantasy look and is one of the clearest themed identity sets announced so far.

updates.title: CookieRun: OvenSmash Updates
updates.subtitle: Track the channels that matter for patch news, event drops, and new costume reveals.
updates.intro: The Help Center does not publish a fixed patch calendar and instead directs players to the official Discord for update timing. The most useful watchlist is Discord for next-update chatter, YouTube and X for reveal posts, and the app store listing for version history.

channels.name: CookieRun: OvenSmash Official Discord
channels.role: Primary place to check next update timing
channels.details: The official Help Center article for update timing specifically tells players to check the CookieRun: OvenSmash Discord regularly for the latest news, tactics, and community updates.
channels.bestFor: Next update notices, live announcements, community conversation

channels.name: CookieRun: OvenSmash Official YouTube
channels.details: The official YouTube channel publishes costume reveal content, including Cosmic Odyssey and Summer Time Breeze videos.

channels.name: CookieRun: OvenSmash Official X
channels.details: The official X feed is used for costume promotions, share campaigns, and seasonal reveal posts, including summer and cosmic-themed costume announcements.
channels.bestFor: Short-form announcements, event reminders, promotional drops

channels.name: CookieRun: OvenSmash App Store Version History
channels.details: The current version history shows Version 1.0.01 with the note: Fixed bugs and registered new in-app products for review.
channels.bestFor: Bug-fix confirmations and store-side version tracking

channels.name: CookieRun: OvenSmash Help Center Update Page
channels.details: The support article on update timing is the official place that points users toward the main live-news channel.
channels.bestFor: Official confirmation of where update news is routed

troubleshooting.title: CookieRun: OvenSmash Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.subtitle: Fix install, update, connection, stutter, crash, and notification issues with the official device steps.
troubleshooting.intro: Most CookieRun: OvenSmash support steps repeat the same core checks: use the official store build, keep the device OS updated, free up space and memory, and make sure the device meets minimum requirements. For iPhone and iPad, iOS 15 or newer is required; for Android, version 9.0 or newer with 3 GB RAM or more.

items.question: I can't install CookieRun: OvenSmash
items.answer: Download the game only from the App Store or Google Play Store. Then check storage, free memory by removing unused apps or media, update the device OS, and confirm the minimum requirements: iOS 15 or newer, or Android 9.0 or newer with 3 GB RAM or more.
steps: Use the official store version only
Check device storage capacity
Free memory by removing unused apps, videos, or photos
Update the device OS to the latest version
Confirm system requirements before trying again

items.question: Why can't I update CookieRun: OvenSmash?
items.answer: Updating may require more space than the minimum install size. Free storage, keep at least 3 GB of space available, update the device OS, and confirm the same minimum requirements used for installation.
steps: Free up device memory by removing unused apps or files
Keep at least 3 GB of free space available
Update the device OS to the latest version
Confirm iOS 15+ or Android 9.0+ with 3 GB RAM or more

items.question: I can't connect to CookieRun: OvenSmash. What do I do?
items.answer: Start with network quality. Unstable networks can make CookieRun: OvenSmash unstable, so switch to a reliable connection, confirm you installed the official store version, update the OS, free some device memory, and check the minimum device requirements.
steps: Move to a stable network connection
Make sure the game was downloaded from the App Store or Google Play
Update the device OS
Free up memory by removing unused apps or media
Confirm iOS 15+ or Android 9.0+ with 3 GB RAM or more

items.question: CookieRun: OvenSmash keeps stuttering during play
items.answer: The Help Center points first to network stability, especially because full bars do not always mean a strong connection. Subway travel and crowded places can still cause unstable play. If the network is fine, close background apps to free device memory.
steps: Use a strong network or a stable 3G or 4G connection
Avoid unstable environments such as crowded locations or subway travel if possible
Close other apps running in the background
Try again after freeing memory

items.question: CookieRun: OvenSmash crashes on Android
items.answer: Update Android first, then check connection quality, free at least 3 GB of space, close background apps, and restart the device. Android device variety means some devices may still fall short of recommended performance.
steps: Update Android in Settings > About Device
Use a solid network connection
Free at least 3 GB of space
Close all running apps
Restart the device

items.question: CookieRun: OvenSmash crashes on iOS
items.answer: Update iOS, check available storage, keep at least 3 GB of space free, remove unused apps or media, then close apps and restart the device. Jail-broken devices are not guaranteed to run CookieRun: OvenSmash correctly.
steps: Update iOS in Settings > General > Software Update
Check storage in Settings > General > About
Keep at least 3 GB of free space available
Remove unused apps, videos, or photos if needed
Close running apps and restart the device

items.question: How do I disable CookieRun: OvenSmash push notifications?
items.answer: There are three paths. In the game settings, go to Settings > Notifications and toggle the needed alerts. On iOS, go to Settings > Notifications and select CookieRun: OvenSmash. On Android, go to Settings > Sounds and notifications > Application notifications, then block or unblock notifications for the game.
steps: In the game or general settings, open Notifications and toggle the needed alerts
On iOS, go to Settings > Notifications and select CookieRun: OvenSmash
On Android, go to Settings > Sounds and notifications > Application notifications, then block or unblock game notifications

faq.title: CookieRun: OvenSmash
faq.subtitle: Frequently asked questions about CookieRun: OvenSmash

questions.question: What is CookieRun: OvenSmash?
questions.answer: CookieRun: OvenSmash is a real-time PvP arena game developed by Devsisters. Players control Cookies in fast-paced 3v3 battles and free-for-all modes set in Platter City.

questions.question: Is CookieRun: OvenSmash free to play?
questions.answer: Yes. CookieRun: OvenSmash is free to download and play on iOS, Android, and PC. It includes optional in-app purchases for cosmetic items and progression boosts.

questions.question: What platforms support CookieRun: OvenSmash?
questions.answer: CookieRun: OvenSmash is available on iOS (App Store), Android (Google Play), and PC. Cross-play is supported across all platforms.

questions.question: How many Cookies are in CookieRun: OvenSmash?
questions.answer: CookieRun: OvenSmash launched with 20 playable Cookies, each with unique abilities, roles, and ultimates. More Cookies are expected to be added in future updates.

questions.question: How do I redeem codes in CookieRun: OvenSmash?
questions.answer: Visit the official DevPlay coupon page at coupon.devplay.com, enter your DevPlay account and the code, then restart the game to collect rewards from your mailbox.

questions.question: What are the best Cookies in CookieRun: OvenSmash?
questions.answer: The strongest launch-meta picks include Dark Choco Cookie, Red Velvet Cookie, Madeleine Cookie, and Cherry Cookie. Check the tier list section for the full ranking.

questions.question: How many game modes does CookieRun: OvenSmash have?
questions.answer: CookieRun: OvenSmash has six game modes: Castle Crasher, Drop the Beat, Jelly Race, Team Battle, Cookie Royale, and Gnome Battle.

questions.question: When did CookieRun: OvenSmash launch?
questions.answer: CookieRun: OvenSmash launched globally on March 26, 2026, developed by Devsisters and published in select regions by VNGGames.
